openmeter/customer/service/hooks.purpose: Lifecycle hook implementations for the customer domain — entitlement validation pre-delete guard (entitlementvalidator.go) and subject-to-customer auto-provisioning (subjectcustomer.go). Hooks integrate with the models.ServiceHook[T] registry so the customer package never couples to its callers.

patterns.name: ServiceHook type-alias pair + concrete struct embeds Noop
patterns.description: Each hook file declares a public ServiceHook[T] alias and a Noop alias; the concrete struct embeds the Noop so only overridden lifecycle methods need implementing.
patterns.example: type EntitlementValidatorHook = models.ServiceHook[customer.Customer]; type NoopEntitlementValidatorHook = models.NoopServiceHook[customer.Customer]

patterns.name: Config struct + Validate() + constructor
patterns.description: Every hook is built via a Config with a Validate() (mandatory nil-dependency checks); the constructor calls Validate() first and wraps the error.
patterns.example: func NewEntitlementValidatorHook(config EntitlementValidatorHookConfig) (EntitlementValidatorHook, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid entitlement validator hook config: %w", err) }; ... }

patterns.name: Compile-time interface assertion
patterns.description: Every concrete hook declares a package-level var _ assertion to verify interface satisfaction at compile time.

patterns.name: OTel tracing in non-trivial hook methods
patterns.description: Non-trivial methods start a span via the Config-injected tracer, record errors / add events, and defer span.End.

patterns.name: Loop-prevention via context key
patterns.description: When a hook calls back into customer.Service (Update/Create), it wraps ctx with subjectservicehooks.NewContextWithSkipSubjectCustomer(ctx) to break re-entrant hook cycles.

patterns.name: models.Generic* error types for domain errors
patterns.description: Domain failures return models.NewGenericValidationError / NotFoundError / ConflictError / PreConditionFailedError — never raw errors, since HTTP encoders map by type.
patterns.example: return models.NewGenericValidationError(fmt.Errorf("customer has entitlements, please remove them before deleting the customer"))

patterns.name: Tests built from package constructors (no app/common)
patterns.description: Tests wire adapters and services directly (customeradapter.New, customerservice.New, subjectadapter.New) with testutils.InitPostgresDB and eventbus.NewMock; never import app/common.
patterns.example: db := testutils.InitPostgresDB(t); customerService, _ := customerservice.New(customerservice.Config{Adapter: customerAdapter, Publisher: publisher})

key_file_guides.role: PreDelete hook for customer.Customer — blocks deletion when active entitlements exist; overrides only PreDelete, rest fall through to the embedded Noop.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Uses clock.Now() for the entitlement snapshot; always pass the caller's ctx, never context.Background().

key_file_guides.role: PostCreate/PostUpdate/PostDelete hook for subject.Subject auto-provisioning a matching Customer; houses CustomerProvisioner (reusable create/update logic) and EnsureStripeCustomer.
key_file_guides.watch_for: getCustomerForSubject tries both usage-attribution and key lookup; EnsureCustomer handles key conflicts by omitting the key on create; CmpSubjectCustomer decides whether an update is needed — extend carefully to avoid spurious updates.

key_file_guides.role: Integration tests for CustomerProvisioner against a real Postgres via testutils.InitPostgresDB (create/update/conflict scenarios).
key_file_guides.watch_for: TestEnv is local and independent of app/common; call env.DBSchemaMigrate(t) before using the DB; use t.Context(), not context.Background().

anti_patterns: Calling customer.Service from a hook without subjectservicehooks.NewContextWithSkipSubjectCustomer(ctx) — causes infinite re-entrant hook invocations.
Returning raw fmt.Errorf for domain conditions — callers and HTTP encoders depend on models.Generic* error types.
Importing app/common in test files — breaks isolation and can introduce import cycles.
Embedding a non-Noop base struct — hooks must embed the Noop alias so future interface method additions don't break compilation.
Using context.Background()/context.TODO() inside hook methods — always propagate the caller's ctx.

decisions.decision: Hooks embed models.NoopServiceHook[T] rather than implementing all methods
decisions.rationale: The ServiceHook interface grows as new lifecycle events are added; embedding Noop provides safe defaults so existing hooks don't break on interface expansion.

decisions.decision: CustomerProvisioner is a separate struct from the hook
decisions.rationale: EnsureCustomer/EnsureStripeCustomer are reused outside the hook (e.g. app/stripe provisioning), so extracting them avoids duplication without tying logic to the subject lifecycle.
Subject create/update should not fail when customer provisioning is non-critical in some deployment modes; the flag lets callers warn-and-continue.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new PreDelete hook for the customer lifecycle
